title: ActiveCampaign vs GetResponse (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ActiveCampaign vs GetResponse. See pricing, features, ratings, and which email marketing platform suits your needs.
intro: ActiveCampaign and GetResponse are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. ActiveCampaign excels in CRM and advanced automation, while GetResponse is built for online course creators and webinar hosts. This comparison helps you choose the right tool based on your specific requirements.

comparison_table.tool_a: Businesses needing advanced automation and CRM
comparison_table.tool_b: Online course creators and webinar hosts

comparison_table.tool_a: Lead scoring, site tracking, CRM, SMS, dynamic content
comparison_table.tool_b: Webinars, landing pages, online courses, conversion funnels

choose_a_if: You need advanced CRM and lead scoring capabilities integrated with email marketing
You want sophisticated automation workflows and site tracking features
Your business prioritizes detailed customer relationship management and segmentation
choose_b_if: You're an online course creator or webinar host looking for an all-in-one platform
You want to start free and scale up as your business grows
You need landing pages and conversion funnel tools alongside email marketing

faq.a: It depends on your needs. ActiveCampaign wins for CRM and automation-focused businesses with higher customer ratings (4.5 vs 4.2 on G2). However, GetResponse is better for online educators and webinar hosts who need those specific features included. Choose based on whether you prioritize CRM/automation or content delivery features.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ActiveCampaign or GetResponse?
faq.a: GetResponse is cheaper to start since it offers a free tier supporting up to 500 subscribers, making it ideal for beginners or testing. ActiveCampaign has no free tier and starts at $15/month, but paid GetResponse plans begin at $19/month. For small businesses, GetResponse's free tier provides better value initially.

faq.q: Can I switch from GetResponse to ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support data export and import, though you should plan the migration carefully. Most email marketing tools allow you to export your contact lists and create similar automation workflows in the new platform. ActiveCampaign's CRM features may require manual setup for full functionality since GetResponse doesn't have equivalent CRM depth.
